name: Mother of Unification Studies (MOUS)
description: A 204-participant multimodal dataset for studying language processing, pairing MEG and fMRI as participants read or listened to sentences (versus word-scrambled controls). Because comprehending language is a core channel of human communication, MOUS is a large, openly available resource for the neural basis of communication, combining high temporal (MEG) and spatial (fMRI) resolution in the same cohort.
citation: Schoffelen J-M, Oostenveld R, Lam NHL, et al. (2019). A 204-subject multimodal neuroimaging dataset to study language processing. Scientific Data, 6, 17.
